model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Beta project card
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (308,151) said dx=148 dy=62 conf=0.90 -> (456,213)
  iter 1: at (456,213) said dx=-74 dy=67 conf=0.80 -> (382,280)
  iter 2: at (419,246) said dx=57 dy=-58 conf=0.90 -> (476,188)
  iter 3: at (433,232) said dx=48 dy=-36 conf=0.90 -> (481,196)
  iter 4: at (445,223) said dx=37 dy=-25 conf=0.90 -> (482,198)
  iter 5: at (454,217) said dx=-72 dy=64 conf=0.80 -> (382,281)
  iter 6: at (436,233) said dx=-54 dy=48 conf=0.90 -> (382,281)
  iter 7: at (423,245) said dx=50 dy=-55 conf=0.90 -> (473,190)
  iter 8: at (436,231) said dx=-53 dy=49 conf=0.90 -> (382,280)
  iter 9: at (422,243) said dx=54 dy=-58 conf=0.90 -> (476,185)
  iter 10: at (436,229) said dx=-53 dy=52 conf=0.90 -> (383,281)
  iter 11: at (422,242) said dx=-39 dy=39 conf=0.90 -> (384,281)
  iter 12: at (413,252) said dx=66 dy=-72 conf=0.90 -> (479,180)
  iter 13: at (429,234) said dx=-47 dy=48 conf=0.90 -> (382,282)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to find the center of the "Beta" project card.
The current crosshair position is (417, 245) as indicated by the bottom right text, but the crosshair itself is at (321, 338) according to visual inspection or looking at the blue circle. Wait, the blue circle is a reference.
Looking at the image:
The "Beta" card is the second card in the first row.
Its bounding box seems to be roughly from x=268 to x=496 and y=184 to y=374.
The center of the Beta card would be around x = (268 + 496) / 2 = 382, y = (184 + 374) / 2 = 279.
The red crosshair is at (321, 338) roughly. Let me re-examine.
The blue circle is at (321, 338)? No, the crosshair is *on* the blue circle.
The crosshair is at (321, 338).
Let's find the center of the "Beta" card more accurately.
Beta card:
Top-left: (268, 184)
Bottom-right: (496, 374)
Center: (382, 279)
Current crosshair position is (321, 338).
Offset dx = 382 - 321 = 61
Offset dy = 279 - 338 = -59
Let's look at the previous iterations to see if there's a pattern.
The target seems to be around (382, 281) based on many iterations.
The current crosshair is at (321, 338).
Target center (382, 281).
dx = 382 - 321 = 61
dy = 281 - 338 = -57
Let's re-verify the crosshair position. It's at the center of the blue circle.
The blue circle is at x=321, y=338.
Wait, the red crosshair is the current position. It is indeed at (321, 338).
The target Beta card center is approximately (382, 280).
dx = 382 - 321 = 61
dy = 280 - 338 = -58
Let's check the target again.
The Beta card is the one with the gear icon and "Beta" text.
It spans from x ~ 268 to 496. Center x ~ 382.
It spans from y ~ 184 to 374. Center y ~ 279.
The crosshair is at x=321, y=338.
dx = 382 - 321 = 61
dy = 279 - 338 = -59
I'll use dx=61, dy=-59. Confidence 0.9.
